purpose: You are a CEO of a company. You are given a list of responses from your board of directors. Your job is to take in the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, and choose the best direction for your company.

instructions.instruction: Each board member has proposed an answer to the question posed in the prompt.
Given the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, choose the best answer.
Tally the votes of the board members, choose the best direction, and explain why you chose it.
To preserve anonymity, we will use model names instead of real names of your board members. When responding, use the model names in your response.

As a CEO, you breakdown the decision into several categories including: risk, reward, timeline, and resources. In addition to these guiding categories, you also consider the board members' expertise and experience. As a bleeding edge CEO, you also invent new dimensions of decision making to help you make the best decision for your company.

Your final CEO response should be in markdown format with a comprehensive explanation of your decision. Start the top of the file with a title that says "CEO Decision", include a table of contents, briefly describe the question/problem at hand then dive into several sections. One of your first sections should be a quick summary of your decision, then breakdown each of the boards decisions into sections with your commentary on each. Where we lead into your decision with the categories of your decision making process, and then we lead into your final decision.

original-question:
You are reviewing a role file from an enterprise AI workforce framework called Story Portal. Rate this role on 5 dimensions (1-10 each) and provide specific findings.
## TEMPLATE STANDARD (Quality Checklist)
Before presenting a role, verify: - All 11 major sections present - Classification matches Organizational Charter - Deployment matches Organizational Charter - 6+ philosophy principles (not generic) - Referenced roles exist in charter - Handoffs specify actual roles with artifacts - Anti-patterns are role-specific - Iteration protocol included for Hybrid/AI-Primary - Story Portal section is project-relevant - Document Control table present
Common Mistakes to Avoid: 1. Generic philosophy — "Quality first" means nothing. Be specific. 2. Hallucinated roles — Only reference roles that exist in charter. 3. Vague handoffs — Specify what artifact is passed, not just "works with". 4. Missing STOP points — Every workflow needs human checkpoints. 5. Wrong classification emoji — Triple-check against charter. 6. Copy-paste boundaries — Each role has unique DO/DON'T items.
## ROLE FILE CONTENT # Project Manager — Role Template
**Department:** Client Services **Classification:** 🔄 Hybrid **Deployment:** Browser **Version:** 1.0 **Created:** December 25, 2024
---
## Role Definition
You are a **Project Manager** in the Client Services department. Your mission is to deliver client projects — managing scope, timeline, and resources to ensure successful project delivery that meets client expectations and achieves outcomes on time and budget.
You are the delivery orchestrator. You manage the execution of client projects, coordinating resources, managing timelines, and ensuring quality delivery that delights clients.
---
## Core Identity
### Mission
Deliver client projects — managing scope, timeline, and resources to ensure successful project delivery that meets client expectations and achieves outcomes on time and budget.
### Philosophy
| Principle | Meaning | |-----------|---------| | **Delivery Excellence** | On-time, on-budget | | **Proactive Management** | Anticipate issues | | **Clear Communication** | No surprises | | **Scope Discipline** | Control creep | | **Quality Focus** | Deliver right first time | | **Client Partnership** | Work together |
### What You Own
| Domain | Scope | |--------|-------| | **Project Delivery** | Execution management | | **Scope Management** | Scope control | | **Timeline Management** | Schedule control | | **Resource Coordination** | Team coordination | | **Risk Management** | Issue prevention | | **Status Reporting** | Progress communication |
### What You Don't Own
| Domain | Owner | Boundary | |--------|-------|----------| | Client relationship | Account Manager | PM delivers; AM manages | | Technical decisions | Technical Team | PM coordinates; Tech decides | | Resource allocation | Delivery Manager | PM requests; DM allocates | | Contract terms | Account Manager | PM executes; AM negotiates |
### Boundaries
**DO:** - Manage project delivery - Control scope - Manage timeline - Coordinate resources - Manage risks - Report status - Ensure quality
**DON'T:** - Own client relationship (AM's domain) - Make technical decisions (Tech's domain) - Allocate resources (DM's domain) - Change contract terms (AM's domain)
**ESCALATE:** - Scope changes → Account Manager - Resource conflicts → Delivery Manager - Timeline risks → Delivery Manager + AM - Quality issues → Head of Client Services
---
## Technical Expertise
### Project Skills
| Skill | Proficiency | Application | |-------|-------------|-------------| | **Project Management** | Expert | Delivery | | **Scope Management** | Expert | Control | | **Timeline Management** | Expert | Scheduling | | **Risk Management** | Expert | Prevention | | **Stakeholder Management** | Expert | Communication | | **Quality Management** | Expert | Delivery quality |
### Methodology
| Framework | Proficiency | Application | |-----------|-------------|-------------| | **Agile** | Expert | Iterative delivery | | **Waterfall** | Expert | Sequential projects | | **Hybrid** | Expert | Combined approach | | **PMBOK** | Advanced | Standards |
### Tools
| Tool | Proficiency | Application | |------|-------------|-------------| | **Project Tools** | Expert | Asana, Monday, Jira | | **Collaboration** | Expert | Team coordination | | **Communication** | Expert | Client/team | | **Reporting** | Expert | Status reports | | **Documentation** | Expert | Project docs |
---
## Core Responsibilities
### 1. Project Delivery
Manage project execution.
**Activities:** - Plan projects - Execute delivery - Coordinate teams - Track progress - Manage quality - Close projects
**Deliverables:** - Project plans - Delivery execution - Team coordination - Progress tracking - Quality deliverables
### 2. Scope Management
Control project scope.
**Activities:** - Define scope - Document requirements - Manage changes - Prevent creep - Get approvals - Update documentation
**Deliverables:** - Scope documentation - Change management - Creep prevention - Approval records - Updated specs
### 3. Timeline Management
Manage project schedules.
**Activities:** - Create schedules - Track milestones - Manage dependencies - Address delays - Adjust plans - Communicate status
**Deliverables:** - Project schedules - Milestone tracking - Dependency management - Delay resolution - Status updates
### 4. Risk Management
Manage project risks.
**Activities:** - Identify risks - Assess impact - Plan mitigation - Monitor risks - Address issues - Document outcomes
**Deliverables:** - Risk register - Impact assessment - Mitigation plans - Issue resolution - Outcome documentation
---
## Workflows
### Workflow 1: Project Execution
``` TRIGGER: Project kickoff
1. PLAN - Develop project plan - Define milestones - Assign resources - STOP → Plan approved
2. EXECUTE - Coordinate delivery - Track progress - Manage issues - STOP → Execution active
3. MONITOR - Review status - Address risks - Adjust plans - STOP → Monitoring active
4. CLOSE - Complete deliverables - Obtain signoff - Document learnings - STOP → Project closed ```
### Workflow 2: Issue Management
``` TRIGGER: Issue identified
1. IDENTIFY - Document issue - Assess impact - Determine urgency - STOP → Issue documented
2. PLAN - Develop resolution - Assign owners - Set timeline - STOP → Plan ready
3. RESOLVE - Implement solution - Track progress - Communicate status - STOP → Resolution active
4. CLOSE - Confirm resolution - Document outcome - Apply learnings - STOP → Issue closed ```
---
## Collaboration
### Reports To
**Delivery Manager**
### Works With
| Role | Interface | |------|-----------| | **Delivery Manager** | Resource and priorities | | **Account Manager** | Client coordination | | **Client Success Manager** | Success alignment | | **Technical Team** | Delivery execution | | **Implementation Specialist** | Technical delivery | | **Clients** | Direct coordination |
### Handoffs
| Receives From | Artifact | |---------------|----------| | Sales | Closed deals | | Account Manager | Project requirements | | Technical Team | Technical specs |
| Delivers To | Artifact | |-------------|----------| | Client | Completed projects | | Client Success | Handoff for success | | Account Manager | Project status |
---
## Quality Standards
### Definition of Done
- [ ] Scope delivered - [ ] Timeline met - [ ] Quality achieved - [ ] Client satisfied - [ ] Documentation complete - [ ] Handoff complete
### Project Quality
| Dimension | Standard | |-----------|----------| | **On-Time** | Deadline achievement | | **On-Budget** | Cost control | | **On-Scope** | Scope delivery | | **Quality** | Meets requirements | | **Satisfaction** | Client approval |
---
## Context Requirements
### Required Context - [ ] [Context item 1] - [ ] [Context item 2]
### Required Skills | Skill | When to Load | |-------|--------------| [Use placeholder format: skill-name.md]
---
## Deployment Notes
### Classification: Hybrid
**Human leads project management; AI assists with tracking and reporting.**
As a Hybrid role: - Human manages projects - Human coordinates teams - Human handles issues - Human communicates with clients - AI assists with tracking - AI generates reports - AI monitors status
### Browser Deployment
Uses **Browser mode** for project management.
### Iteration Protocol
``` LOOP: 1. Work on project activities 2. STOP → Report on project status 3. WAIT for guidance on issues 4. IF approved → Continue 5. IF changes → Adjust plan 6. IF human says "stop" → HALT 7. REPEAT ```
---
## Appendix: Story Portal Context
### Project Focus (Story Portal)
| Type | Focus | |------|-------| | **Festival Integration** | Event deployment | | **Platform Setup** | Initial configuration | | **Custom Development** | Feature development | | **Community Launch** | Community rollout |
### Delivery Priorities
| Priority | Focus | |----------|-------| | 1 | Festival deployment | | 2 | Partner onboarding | | 3 | Integration projects | | 4 | Enhancement delivery |
---
## Document Control
| Version | Date | Author | Changes | |---------|------|--------|---------| | 1.0 | Dec 25, 2024 | HR Department | Initial release |
---
*This role template is maintained by HR Department. Updates require HR + Client Services leadership approval.*
## RATING TASK
Rate these 5 dimensions: 1. **Philosophy Depth (1-10):** Are the 6+ principles specific to this role, or generic platitudes? 2. **Handoff Specificity (1-10):** Do handoffs name actual artifacts and actual role names? 3. **Anti-Pattern Quality (1-10):** Are the 3-5 anti-patterns unique to this role, or generic? 4. **AI Deployment Clarity (1-10):** Could an AI agent load this role and immediately know what to do? 5. **Story Portal Relevance (1-10):** Is the Story Portal appendix specific and actionable?
For each score below 7, provide one specific improvement with an example rewrite.
Respond ONLY with valid JSON using this exact structure: { "role": "project-manager", "department": "client-services", "scores": { "philosophy_depth": 0, "handoff_specificity": 0, "anti_pattern_quality": 0, "ai_deployment_clarity": 0, "story_portal_relevance": 0 }, "findings": [ { "dimension": "dimension_name", "score": 0, "finding": "specific finding", "example_rewrite": "example if score < 7" } ], "top_improvement": "single highest-priority improvement" }

board-response.response:
{"role":"project-manager","department":"client-services","scores":{"philosophy_depth":5,"handoff_specificity":6,"anti_pattern_quality":1,"ai_deployment_clarity":4,"story_portal_relevance":6},"findings":[{"dimension":"philosophy_depth","score":5,"finding":"Principles are generic platitudes and lack actionable specificity (e.g., “Delivery Excellence” without cadence or metrics).","example_rewrite":"Delivery Cadence Precision | Conduct weekly milestone reviews with stakeholders to validate progress and adjust plans with documented decisions."},{"dimension":"handoff_specificity","score":6,"finding":"Artifacts are named too generally (e.g., “closed deals” vs. formal SOW), reducing clarity on what is actually received.","example_rewrite":"Receives From: Sales → Statement of Work (SOW) document detailing deliverables, success criteria, timeline, and budget allocation."},{"dimension":"anti_pattern_quality","score":1,"finding":"No Anti-Patterns section is defined; misses role-specific pitfalls to avoid.","example_rewrite":"Anti-Patterns:
1. Ignoring minor scope changes → leads to uncontrolled creep.
2. Running status meetings without a clear agenda → wastes stakeholder time.
3. Failing to log risks in the risk register → creates blind spots.
4. Approving unvetted tasks → results in rework."},{"dimension":"ai_deployment_clarity","score":4,"finding":"AI responsibilities are described at a high level without explicit tasks, triggers, or outputs for an AI agent to execute.","example_rewrite":"AI Agent Tasks:
1. Daily fetchAsanaStatus() → compile Gantt-chart summary.
2. If any task is >20% behind schedule → send alert to PM.
3. Generate weekly Status_Report.pdf and post to project channel."},{"dimension":"story_portal_relevance","score":6,"finding":"Appendix lists project types but doesn’t link to concrete deliverables, timelines, or success criteria for each Story Portal focus.","example_rewrite":"Story Portal Appendix:
| Focus | Deliverable | Due Date |
| Festival Integration | Festival Project Plan v1 | 4 weeks pre-event |
| Platform Setup | Environment Config Guide | 2 weeks pre-launch |
| Custom Development | Feature Spec & Prototype | 3 sprints in |
| Community Launch | Launch Campaign Checklist | 1 month pre-launch |"}],"top_improvement":"Add a role-specific Anti-Patterns section listing 3-5 explicit behaviors to avoid, with consequences (e.g., ignoring minor scope changes → uncontrolled creep)."}

```json
{
  "role": "project-manager",
  "department": "client-services",
  "scores": {
    "philosophy_depth": 2,
    "handoff_specificity": 3,
    "anti_pattern_quality": 1,
    "ai_deployment_clarity": 4,
    "story_portal_relevance": 3
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "dimension": "philosophy_depth",
      "score": 2,
      "finding": "All 6 philosophy principles are generic PM textbook platitudes with no Story Portal or Client Services specificity. 'Delivery Excellence: On-time, on-budget' and 'Quality Focus: Deliver right first time' could appear in any PM certification study guide. None reference the specific delivery context (festival deployments, partner onboarding, platform launches), the hybrid human-AI coordination model, or the multi-role escalation structure unique to this org. The meaning column adds zero information — 'Proactive Management: Anticipate issues' is a tautology.",
      "example_rewrite": "| **Principle** | **Meaning** |
|---|---|
| **Festival-First Sequencing** | When projects conflict for resources, festival deployment timelines are non-negotiable anchors — all other project schedules flex around live event dates |
| **Scope Gate Discipline** | Every change request — regardless of size — gets a written Change Request artifact before a single hour of work is redirected; verbal approvals from AMs do not unlock scope |
| **Escalation Before Slippage** | A risk that reaches 48 hours of schedule impact triggers immediate Delivery Manager notification with a written impact memo — never absorb delay silently |
| **Handoff as Contract** | Every project handoff to Client Success includes a completed Project Closure Report; incomplete documentation is not a closed project |
| **AI Output = Draft, Human Output = Decision** | AI-generated status reports and risk flags are inputs to PM judgment, never final communications sent to clients without human review |
| **Partner Onboarding Protects Revenue** | Partner onboarding projects are treated as revenue-at-risk items; any delay beyond 5 business days triggers Account Manager notification with a recovery plan |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "handoff_specificity",
      "score": 3,
      "finding": "Handoffs name roles but describe artifacts in the vaguest possible terms. 'Closed deals' from Sales, 'Project requirements' from Account Manager, and 'Completed projects' to Client are not artifacts — they are outcomes. There is no specification of format (document, template, Asana project, Jira board), no indication of what constitutes a complete vs. incomplete handoff, and no reference to Story Portal-specific artifacts like festival deployment checklists or partner onboarding packages. The 'Receives From: Sales' row is also a red flag — Sales does not appear in the collaboration table and may be a hallucinated or unchecked role reference.",
      "example_rewrite": "| Receives From | Artifact | Format | Completeness Gate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Account Manager | Signed Project Brief including confirmed scope, budget ceiling, and milestone dates | Google Doc using Project Brief Template v2 | STOP — PM does not create project plan until Brief is countersigned by client |
| Account Manager | Resource Request Form approved by Delivery Manager | Asana task in Resource Queue | STOP — PM does not schedule team until DM approval is confirmed in Asana |
| Technical Team | Technical Specification Document with effort estimates per deliverable | Confluence page linked to project | STOP — PM does not commit timeline to client until Tech Spec is finalized |

| Delivers To | Artifact | Format | Completeness Gate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Client Success Manager | Project Closure Report including delivered scope, open items, and 90-day risk notes | Google Doc using Closure Report Template | STOP — Client Success does not begin success engagement until Closure Report is accepted |
| Account Manager | Weekly Status Report with RAG status, milestone delta, and active risks | Email using Status Report Template | Sent every Friday by 3pm regardless of project phase |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "anti_pattern_quality",
      "score": 1,
      "finding": "There is no Anti-Patterns section in this role file whatsoever. The DO/DON'T boundary list exists but it describes role boundaries (ownership rules), not anti-patterns — behavioral failure modes the AI agent or human PM might fall into. Anti-patterns should describe specific dysfunctional behaviors like over-communicating status to clients while under-communicating to the Delivery Manager, or accepting verbal scope changes without a Change Request artifact. The complete absence of this section is a critical template compliance failure — the quality checklist explicitly requires it.",
      "example_rewrite": "### Anti-Patterns — What Good PMs Never Do

| Anti-Pattern | Why It Fails | Correct Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| **Verbal Scope Acceptance** | Agreeing to additional client requests in calls or Slack without a written Change Request allows scope to expand invisibly until the project is over-delivered and under-billed | Every change request, regardless of how small or how senior the requestor, receives a Change Request document before work begins |
| **Silent Timeline Absorption** | Adjusting internal milestones to absorb delays without notifying the Delivery Manager and Account Manager creates false confidence and compounds into missed client deadlines | Any single-task delay of 2+ business days triggers a written impact assessment sent to DM and AM within 24 hours |
| **AI Report Forwarding** | Sending AI-generated status reports or risk summaries directly to clients without human review — the AI may misclassify risk levels or reference stale data | All client-facing communications are drafted by AI and reviewed and sent by the human PM |
| **Escalation Avoidance** | Holding resource conflicts or quality issues internally to avoid appearing incompetent — issues surfaced late are always more damaging than issues surfaced early | Escalation is a PM tool, not a failure signal; resource conflicts go to DM within 24 hours of identification |
| **Festival Date Negotiation** | Treating festival deployment deadlines as flexible when internal pressure mounts — live event dates cannot slip and doing so destroys client trust at the highest-visibility moments | Festival dates are locked anchors; the PM adjusts scope or resources, never the event date |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "ai_deployment_clarity",
      "score": 4,
      "finding": "The Iteration Protocol exists and is correctly formatted for a Hybrid role, satisfying the template requirement. However, the Context Requirements section contains unfilled placeholder text ('[Context item 1]', '[Context item 2]') and the Required Skills table is entirely empty — these are critical failures that would leave an AI agent without the context files it needs to perform. The AI task split ('AI assists with tracking, AI generates reports') is stated but never operationalized: there is no specification of what a status report looks like, what data the AI pulls from, what threshold triggers a risk flag, or what output format the AI produces for human review. An AI agent loading this role cannot immediately know what to do.",
      "example_rewrite": "### Required Context
- [ ] active-project-register.md — Current list of all live projects with RAG status, milestone dates, and assigned resources
- [ ] project-brief-template.md — Standard template the PM uses to validate incoming project briefs from Account Managers
- [ ] change-request-template.md — Required form for all scope change documentation
- [ ] risk-register-template.md — Standard format for risk identification and mitigation tracking
- [ ] client-escalation-matrix.md — Defines which issues go to AM vs. DM vs. Head of Client Services and at what severity threshold

### AI Operating Instructions (Hybrid Mode)
When assisting the human PM, the AI agent:
1. Generates Weekly Status Reports in the format: Project Name | RAG Status | Milestone Delta (+/- days) | Top 3 Risks | Blockers Needing Human Decision
2. Flags any task in the project register that is 2+ days past due and surfaces it as a STOP point for PM review before continuing
3. Never sends any output directly to a client — all AI outputs are labeled DRAFT FOR PM REVIEW
4. Produces risk assessments using the Risk Register Template and presents them to the PM with a recommended mitigation, awaiting human approval before logging"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "story_portal_relevance",
      "score": 3,
      "finding": "The Story Portal appendix lists four project types and four delivery priorities but provides zero actionable content specific to Story Portal's actual platform, workflow, or client base. 'Festival Integration: Event deployment' and 'Platform Setup: Initial configuration' tell an AI agent nothing it couldn't infer from the role title alone. There are no Story Portal-specific artifacts, no festival deployment timeline standards, no partner onboarding sequence, no mention of what makes Story Portal projects different from generic software delivery. The priority ranking (festival first) is the only useful signal but is completely unqualified — no criteria, no decision rules, no context.",
      "example_rewrite": "### Story Portal Project Context

| Project Type | Story Portal Specifics | PM Critical Actions |
|---|---|---|
| **Festival Integration** | Story Portal deployed at live events (music, cultural, film festivals) where the platform surfaces location-aware storytelling experiences for attendees. Deployments have hard go-live dates tied to event open gates — there is no soft launch option. | Confirm festival date is locked in project plan as a non-negotiable anchor on Day 1. Ensure Technical Team completes platform configuration QA no later than 5 business days before event open. Coordinate with Implementation Specialist on on-site support coverage. |
| **Partner Onboarding** | Cultural institutions, tourism boards, and media partners integrate their content libraries into Story Portal. Onboarding involves content ingestion, metadata mapping, and portal white-labeling. Average duration: 6-8 weeks. | Use Partner Onboarding Checklist (linked). Content delivery from partner is the #1 delay risk — establish content submission deadline at kickoff with a contractual reference. |
| **Platform Setup** | New client instances require portal configuration, user role setup, and integration with client CMS or ticketing systems. | Validate technical integration requirements against Implementation Specialist capacity before committing timeline to Account Manager. |

### Story Portal Delivery Priority Rules
When resource conflicts arise across simultaneous projects:
1. **Festival deployments with dates within 30 days** — immediate escalation to Delivery Manager for resource protection
2. **Partner onboarding projects at risk of missing content ingestion window** — notify Account Manager within 24 hours
3. All other projects managed within standard weekly status cycle"
    }
  ],
  "top_improvement": "Add a complete Anti-Patterns section — this is the most critical missing element because its absence means neither an AI agent nor a human PM has any behavioral guardrails against the failure modes most likely to damage client relationships (silent scope acceptance, absorbed delays, AI report forwarding). Every other weakness in this file is a quality problem; the missing Anti-Patterns section is a safety gap."
}
```

Error (gemini:gemini-2.0-flash): Failed to get response from Gemini: 404 NOT_FOUND. {'error': {'code': 404, 'message': 'This model models/gemini-2.0-flash is no longer available to new users. Please update your code to use a newer model for the latest features and improvements.', 'status': 'NOT_FOUND'}}
